# Pushing the Jira stories with a lighter-weight model (Haiku or similar)

Copy-paste prompts for a lighter/cheaper model (e.g. Claude Haiku) to create the Jira issues from the
generated CSVs. Jira only — Confluence is a separate job, see
[`PUSH-TO-JIRA-CONFLUENCE.md`](PUSH-TO-JIRA-CONFLUENCE.md). Use this variant when the model doing the
push is weaker than the one authoring the artifacts and needs smaller batches + more explicit
bookkeeping to avoid drift over a long run.

**The artifact already carries everything it needs** (regenerated by `generate_jira.py`), so the
prompts stay simple and tool-independent:

- **Description is minimal by design** — Acceptance Criteria + a back-link, nothing else (Current
  Behaviour, Target implementation, Test Cases stay on GitHub, linked not duplicated — see
  `PUSH-TO-JIRA-CONFLUENCE.md`'s explanation of why). This also means each row is short, which is
  exactly what keeps a weaker model accurate over a long batch run — there's less to transcribe per
  row and less to drift on.
- **One shared epic per lane — already created (or create it in step 1).** `Federate BreakDown
  Product` (backend) and `Federate BreakDown Product — Frontend` (frontend) — every Story/Spike row's
  `Epic Link` column points at one of them by name. Have the keys ready as `<BE_EPIC_KEY>` /
  `<FE_EPIC_KEY>` once created.
- **Domain-tagged summaries** — each `Summary` is already `(<Domain>) <title> [<Story ID>]` (backend)
  or `[<Domain> FE] <title> [<Story ID>]` (frontend). Nothing to prefix at push time.
- **Source:** `finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv` = 1 Epic row + **208 Story rows + 8 program-Spike
  rows** (216 total) across all **8 phase-1 domains** (product, bom, claims, measurement, impression,
  productDetails, packaging, watchlist — the remaining domains join in a later phase and aren't in
  this file yet). Per-domain files (`bom.csv`, `product.csv`, …) carry the same shared epic and hold
  only that domain's Story rows (backend + frontend, no spikes).
- **The 8 program spikes** (`SPIKE-01`…`SPIKE-07` plus any added since) are cross-domain — they live
  **only in `all-stories.csv`** (Issue Type=Spike), not in any per-domain file. If you push
  per-domain, push the spikes once too (see below).
- **Complex cross-domain cases import separately** — each `output/complexStories/<case>/<case>.csv`
  is imported under its home-stub story, not via `all-stories.csv`.

> **Tooling is unknown / not Atlassian-specific.** Your org's push tool might be the Atlassian MCP, a
> custom tool, or Copilot's own Jira integration. **These prompts don't name any tool** — the agent
> uses whatever Jira create/search/link capability it has. Step 0 makes it discover that first.

---

## Do we do one prompt for all domains?

You can, but 216 issues in one run is enough for a lighter model to drift (skip a row, mis-map a
dependency). The prompts below default to **per-domain** (largest domain, product, is 79 rows;
smallest, impression, is 9) — smaller context per run, cheaper, and if one domain fails you re-drive
just that one. Every domain links to the **same** two epics and appends to the **same**
`jira-key-map.csv`, so the domains add up cleanly.

---

## Option A — One open instruction, per domain (run once per domain)

Paste this once per domain. Replace `<PROJECT_KEY>`, `<BE_EPIC_KEY>` / `<FE_EPIC_KEY>` (existing
epics — create them on the first run if they don't exist yet), `<DOMAIN>` (e.g. `bom`, `product` —
the file name without `.csv`), and `target-corp/saritha-mathai-repositories-research`.

```
You are pushing ONE domain of our migration backlog into Jira project <PROJECT_KEY>. Source file:
finalArtifacts/jira/<DOMAIN>.csv. Work autonomously through this whole file; only stop if a tool is
missing or you hit an error you can't resolve.

SETUP
- List the Jira tools you actually have (create issue, search issues, link issues) and confirm the
  project has an issue-link type meaning "Blocks / is blocked by". If a "Spike" issue type doesn't
  exist, create Spike rows as Story with an extra label "spike". Tell me what you found, then continue.
- Find-or-create the two epics named in the CSV's two Issue Type=Epic rows ("Federate BreakDown
  Product" for backend, "Federate BreakDown Product — Frontend" for frontend) — reuse if they already
  exist (they're shared across all domains), only create on the very first domain you push.

CSV columns: Issue Type, Story ID, Summary, Epic Name, Epic Link, Phase, T-shirt size, Labels,
Labels, Labels, Parent Link, Depends On, External Dependency, Status, Description.

1. STORIES/SPIKES: for every row with Issue Type=Story or Spike (process in batches of ~15 so you
   stay accurate):
   - issue type per the row (Spike -> Spike type, else Story-with-"spike"-label).
   - summary = the Summary column VERBATIM (already carries the domain tag and Story ID — do not
     change it).
   - description = the Description column's Acceptance Criteria list, PLUS rewrite its "Full story:"
     line from a relative path into a real URL:
     "Full story: https://github.com/target-corp/saritha-mathai-repositories-research/blob/main/output/analysis/<DOMAIN>/be-04-stories.md#<Story ID>"
     Preserve the numbered-list formatting; do not collapse it to one line or strip the numbering.
     Do NOT add Current Behaviour, Target, or Test Cases — the CSV deliberately omits them (they stay
     on GitHub, linked).
   - labels = the three Labels values, plus "phase-<Phase>", "size-<T-shirt size>", and
     "id-<Story ID>" (e.g. id-BOM-BE-S-01). The id- label is required.
   - link the issue to the correct epic (backend rows -> <BE_EPIC_KEY>, frontend rows -> <FE_EPIC_KEY>).
   - IDEMPOTENT: before creating, search for label "id-<Story ID>"; if it exists, skip (don't
     duplicate).
   - After each batch, append "<Story ID>,<Jira Key>" lines to
     finalArtifacts/jira/jira-key-map.csv (header "Story ID,Jira Key" if new). This file accumulates
     across all domains — never overwrite it.

2. DEPENDENCIES (after this domain's issues exist): for every row with a non-empty "Depends On", read
   the dependent's key and each dependency's key from finalArtifacts/jira/jira-key-map.csv, then
   create a "Blocks" link (dependency blocks the dependent). Depends On sometimes uses short ids like
   "B-01" — match by suffix within this domain (<TOKEN>-BE-B-01). If an id isn't in the map yet (it
   may belong to a domain not pushed yet), record it as unresolved; never guess a key. Skip links that
   already exist.

3. VERIFY & REPORT: count issues you created/skipped for this domain vs the Story/Spike rows in
   finalArtifacts/jira/<DOMAIN>.csv; list any row with no issue and any Depends On entry with no link
   (note which unresolved ids point at another domain). Give me the counts and the gap list.
```

**Run order — repeat for each domain** (any order; counts = backend + frontend Story rows, no
spikes):

| `<DOMAIN>` | rows | | `<DOMAIN>` | rows |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bom` | 44 | | `packaging` | 28 |
| `claims` | 24 | | `product` | 79 |
| `impression` | 9 | | `productDetails` | 15 |
| `measurement` | 34 | | `watchlist` | 16 |

> **Rows include both backend and frontend for that domain** (per-domain files are combined — see
> `PUSH-TO-JIRA-CONFLUENCE.md`). The **program spikes** aren't in any per-domain file — after the
> domains, push them once: run the Option-A prompt against `finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv` with
> the idempotent skip (it re-creates nothing that already has an `id-…` label, so only the `SPIKE-0x`
> rows land — `all-stories.csv` has no frontend rows, backend-only + spikes).

**After the last domain — one cross-domain sweep** (catches any dependency that pointed at a domain
pushed later, which was "unresolved" at the time):
```
All 8 domains are pushed. Do a final dependency pass over finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv using
finalArtifacts/jira/jira-key-map.csv: for every "Depends On" that has no Jira link yet, create the
"Blocks" link now (both keys should be in the map). Report links added and anything still unresolved.
```

> Want it all in one shot instead? Same prompt, but set the source to
> `finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv` (one run, 216 backend + spike rows — frontend rows are
> per-domain-file only, there is no combined all-frontend file inside `finalArtifacts/`). Riskier for
> a lighter model over 200+ issues — the per-domain runs above are steadier.

---

## Option B — Step by step (same work, review between phases)

Use these if you want to check each phase before the next. Per-domain: swap `all-stories.csv` for a
single domain file (e.g. `finalArtifacts/jira/bom.csv`) but keep linking to the **same** epics.

**Step 0 — discover tools**
```
List the Jira tools you have (create issue, search, link issues). Confirm project <PROJECT_KEY> has a
"Blocks / is blocked by" link type, and whether it has a "Spike" issue type (if not we'll use Story +
label "spike"). Report and STOP.
```

**Step 1 — confirm/create the epics**
```
Find-or-create "Federate BreakDown Product" (backend) and "Federate BreakDown Product — Frontend"
(frontend) in <PROJECT_KEY>. Report the two keys (<BE_EPIC_KEY> / <FE_EPIC_KEY>) and STOP.
```

**Step 2 — create stories/spikes (repeat per batch)**
```
Create Jira issues for rows 2–16 of finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv (skip Epic rows), in
<PROJECT_KEY>, linked to the correct epic per row (backend -> <BE_EPIC_KEY>, frontend -> <FE_EPIC_KEY>).
- summary = the Summary column verbatim.
- description = the Acceptance Criteria list from the Description column, plus the "Full story:" link
  rewritten as a GitHub URL (see PUSH-TO-JIRA-CONFLUENCE.md's rewrite rule). Preserve numbered-list
  formatting; never flatten or strip it. Do not add content not already in the CSV.
- labels = the three Labels + "phase-<Phase>" + "size-<T-shirt size>" + "id-<Story ID>".
- Spike rows -> Spike type (or Story + label "spike").
- Before each: search label "id-<Story ID>"; skip if it exists.
- Append "<Story ID>,<Jira Key>" per issue to finalArtifacts/jira/jira-key-map.csv.
- Ignore Depends On for now.
Report: Story ID | domain | key | created/skipped. STOP.
```
Repeat with the next batch of rows until done, then:
```
Confirm finalArtifacts/jira/jira-key-map.csv has 216 data lines and list any Story IDs from
all-stories.csv missing from it.
```

**Step 3 — link dependencies**
```
Using only finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv (Depends On) and finalArtifacts/jira/jira-key-map.csv
(Story ID -> key): for each row with a non-empty Depends On, create a "Blocks" link where each
dependency blocks the row. Match short ids like "B-01" by suffix within the same domain. Unresolvable
ids -> report, don't guess. Skip links that already exist. Report links created and any unresolved
ids. STOP.
```

**Step 4 — verify**
```
Count issues linked to the two epics vs the 216 Story/Spike rows. List any CSV row with no issue and
any Depends On with no link. Report gaps only.
```

---

## Re-sync after regenerating the CSVs

Safe and non-duplicating because every issue has an `id-<Story ID>` label and the map file exists:

```
The CSVs were regenerated. Re-sync finalArtifacts/jira/all-stories.csv into <PROJECT_KEY> without
duplicates: for each Story/Spike row, search "id-<Story ID>" — if it exists, update its
summary/description/labels in place; if not, create it (as above) and append to jira-key-map.csv.
Skip Epic rows. Then add only missing dependency links. Report created/updated/unchanged counts.
```

---

## Notes

- **Formatting:** since `generate_jira.py` emits a short numbered Acceptance Criteria list plus a
  back-link, any re-push must keep that structure — a re-sync that flattens the numbered list is a
  regression, not a no-op.
- **Why two passes (create, then link):** Depends On references our Story IDs, which have no Jira key
  until the issue exists. The `jira-key-map.csv` file is the bridge — keep it; it's also what later
  wires Confluence pages to live Jira status.
- **Per-domain run:** BOM = 44 rows (BE+FE combined), Product = 79 — **no spikes in per-domain
  files** (the program spikes are in `all-stories.csv` only). Smaller context per run, cheaper,
  easier to re-drive if one domain fails — but every domain still links to the same two epics.
- **If your tool is a native CSV importer** (not an agent): import `all-stories.csv` — domain-tagged
  summaries, labels, and the `Epic Link` (by name) are all in the file, so rows attach to the existing
  epic. **Delete the `Issue Type=Epic` row first** if the epic already exists (importing it risks a
  duplicate). Only the Depends On links and the GitHub-URL rewrite need the agent afterwards
  (importers can't resolve our-ID → Jira-key or rewrite the relative back-link into a URL).
